goal: Continue the sidebar-navigation refinement with meaningful badge counts and better first-entry landing behavior.
deliverable: Subview badges in the left sidebar where counts carry signal
Action-first default landing for operations and reports
Small sidebar rendering polish that keeps the shell readable
Validation proving the changes do not break the current baseline
constraints: Keep the existing first-level navigation structure
Use lightweight local state for badges instead of new backend endpoints
Avoid turning every submenu item into a noisy counter
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar submenu badges render from live state for meaningful subviews
Operations defaults to procurement on first entry when no subview is already chosen
Reports defaults to tracker on first entry when no subview is already chosen
Remembered subviews and explicit hashes still override defaults
Baseline validation and touched-file syntax checks pass
product_acceptance_criteria: Users can tell where work is concentrated before opening a subview
First entry into the heaviest workspaces lands closer to actual day-to-day usage
manual_spot_checks: Open operations without a subview hash and confirm it lands on procurement
Open reports without a subview hash and confirm it lands on tracker
Confirm tracker, procurement, and exceptions badges appear when their queues are non-empty
Confirm explicit hashes such as #/reports/suppliers still open the requested subview
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after validation passes and the default-landing plus badge logic is wired into the shell.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the sidebar badge and default-landing work that was actually implemented and verified.
non_goals: A full inbox or saved-filter system
inferred_assumptions: The user's 'continue' request means continuing the current sidebar-navigation optimization line.
Action-first defaults are a useful next step as long as explicit user navigation still wins.
